canonical_definition: The emergent ability of a system to learn effective, generalizable representations and perform well on complex downstream tasks, hypothesized to arise from exposure to and prediction of complex data.
papers: Intelligence at the Edge of Chaos
The KoLMogorov Test: Compression by Code Generation
